skill.name: competitor-analysis-bot
skill.description: 竞品自动分析 — 输入竞品名单→自动采集数据+SWOT分析+差距报告
skill.summary: 竞品自动分析 — 输入竞品名单→自动采集数据+SWOT分析+差距报告
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md:4 is a false positive caused by metadata text, not a cryptographic implementation. The high entropy finding at SKILL.md:1 is also a false positive because the file is plain Markdown with Chinese text and no encoded payload, scripts, network calls, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:4, but the line is a Markdown front matter description for competitor analysis. No cryptographic algorithm, encryption routine, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only a product description in front matter. There is no executable code or reference to a cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:1 for entropy, but the file is readable Markdown with YAML front matter and Chinese text. No binary blob, encrypted content, encoded command, or hidden payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed plain text content across the file. The entropy signal is plausibly caused by non-ASCII Chinese characters, not obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Competitive research can take hours when sources, features, and positioning are scattered. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to compare competitors, summarize SWOT findings, and produce a gap report.
actual_capabilities: Turns a competitor list into a structured research plan.
Organizes public competitor information into comparable categories.
Produces SWOT summaries for each named competitor.
Identifies product, pricing, positioning, and feature gaps.
Drafts a concise competitor analysis report for review.
limitations: It depends on the AI tool and available browsing or provided source material.
It cannot guarantee that third-party market data is current or complete.
It does not replace legal, financial, or executive review.
It may need human fact-checking for pricing, traffic, and private company claims.

use_cases.title: Plan a Product Launch
use_cases.description: Compare direct competitors before defining launch messaging, pricing, and feature priorities.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Sales Battlecard
use_cases.description: Summarize competitor strengths, weaknesses, and objection handling points for customer-facing teams.

use_cases.title: Assess Market Position
use_cases.description: Build a practical gap report that highlights opportunities and risks in a target segment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Competitor Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a short list of known competitors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these competitors: [competitor names]. Compare their target users, main features, pricing, strengths, weaknesses, and positioning.

prompt_templates.title: SWOT Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a structured view of each competitor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SWOT analysis for [competitor names]. Use only the sources I provide or clearly label any assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [our product] against [competitor names]. Identify feature gaps, messaging gaps, pricing gaps, and three actionable opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Executive Competitive Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an executive competitor analysis for [market]. Include competitors, evidence quality, SWOT summaries, gap analysis, risks, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Analyze three project management tools for a small agency.
output_examples.output: A comparison table, SWOT notes for each tool, pricing observations, and a short list of gaps a small agency could exploit.

output_examples.input: Compare our analytics product with two enterprise competitors.
output_examples.output: A report covering target customers, major differentiators, missing capabilities, positioning risks, and recommended messaging angles.

output_examples.input: Create a sales battlecard for a named competitor.
output_examples.output: A concise battlecard with competitor strengths, weaknesses, likely objections, response points, and evidence notes.

best_practices: Provide competitor names, market segment, and your own product context.
Attach trusted sources when accuracy matters.
Ask the model to separate verified facts from assumptions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated market claims as verified facts without review.
Do not include confidential competitor data that you are not allowed to use.
Do not ask for legal or investment decisions based only on the report.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It helps compare competitors, summarize SWOT findings, and create gap reports from provided or available information.

faq.question: Does it collect live web data?
faq.answer: It can guide analysis using the host AI tool capabilities. Live data depends on whether that tool has browsing or connected sources.

faq.question: Can it analyze private companies?
faq.answer: Yes, but results may be limited when reliable public data is scarce.

faq.question: Is the output ready for executive use?
faq.answer: It can produce an executive-style brief, but key claims should be reviewed before business decisions.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide competitor names, your product context, target customers, market region, and any trusted source links.
